perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE has been hired by FRANKLIN in a position with access to information relating to the understanding of, testing, or improvement of existing products of FRANKLIN, the development of new products for FRANKLIN, and/or the general business activities of FRANKLIN.
perturbation.changed_text: STAFF has been hired by FRANKLIN in a position with access to information relating to the understanding of, testing, or improvement of existing products of FRANKLIN, the development of new products for FRANKLIN, and/or the general business activities of FRANKLIN.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'EMPLOYEE' is replaced with 'STAFF'. While seemingly synonymous, this introduces ambiguity. Throughout the agreement, 'EMPLOYEE' is used extensively to define obligations and rights. By using 'STAFF' here, it is unclear if all subsequent references to 'EMPLOYEE' also include 'STAFF', or if a distinction is intended. This contradicts the rest of the agreement, which is focused on employee obligations, and here the obligations are with the Staff member. Therefore, the in-text contradiction occurs on which entity this contract it is trying to establish an aggrement with.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In and for the consideration of the employment of EMPLOYEE by FRANKLIN, EMPLOYEE agrees as follows: 1. EMPLOYEE shall promptly and fully disclose to FRANKLIN any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements made by him or her pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN during his or her period of employment by FRANKLIN, and any improvements to his or her invention, writings, programs, and discoveries, made, conceived or acquired by him or her no later than one year after the termination of employment, whether made or conceived solely or jointly with others, whether during regular business hours or otherwise; said Inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements shall become and remain the property of FRANKLIN whether or not patent applications or copyright, trademark, or maskwork registrations are filed thereon. 2. EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of FRANKLIN, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and any and all foreign countries, on said inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, without further consideration. 3. EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request of FRANKLIN execute all papers and do all other things that may be reasonably required in order to protect the rights of FRANKLIN, and to vest in FRANKLIN or its successors or assigns the entire rights, title and interest in and to any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, program improvements, and applications for letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations relating to anything pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN as provided above.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'EMPLOYEE', which is subsequently referenced throughout the rest of the agreement, including in the contradicted text, to define obligations and rights. The changed text replaces 'EMPLOYEE' with 'STAFF' in one instance. Because the rest of the agreement uses 'EMPLOYEE' without defining 'STAFF' as equivalent, it creates ambiguity and a direct conflict regarding which entity the obligations apply to. The contradicted text specifically refers to 'EMPLOYEE', and it's unclear whether 'STAFF' is also bound by those obligations, creating a strong in-text contradiction based on misaligned terminology as per the provided definition.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE shall promptly and fully disclose to FRANKLIN any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements made by him or her pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN during his or her period of employment by FRANKLIN, and any improvements to his or her invention, writings, programs, and discoveries, made, conceived or acquired by him or her no later than one year after the termination of employment, whether made or conceived solely or jointly with others, whether during regular business hours or otherwise; said Inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements shall become and remain the property of FRANKLIN whether or not patent applications or copyright, trademark, or maskwork registrations are filed thereon.
perturbation.changed_text: WORKER shall promptly and fully disclose to FRANKLIN any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements made by him or her pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN during his or her period of employment by FRANKLIN, and any improvements to his or her invention, writings, programs, and discoveries, made, conceived or acquired by him or her no later than six months after the termination of employment, whether made or conceived solely or jointly with others, whether during regular business hours or otherwise; said Inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements shall become and remain the property of FRANKLIN whether or not patent applications or copyright, trademark, or maskwork registrations are filed thereon.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'EMPLOYEE' is replaced with 'WORKER' and 'one year' with 'six months'. Throughout the agreement, 'EMPLOYEE' is used extensively to define obligations and rights. By using 'WORKER' here, it is unclear if all subsequent references to 'EMPLOYEE' also include 'WORKER', or if a distinction is intended. Also, the text contradicts itself. the employee needs to disclose the work made 1 year after the termination and 6 months after the termination. This creates contradictory requirements for when these disclosures need to occur.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE shall promptly and fully disclose to FRANKLIN any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements made by him or her pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN during his or her period of employment by FRANKLIN, and any improvements to his or her invention, writings, programs, and discoveries, made, conceived or acquired by him or her no later than one year after the termination of employment, whether made or conceived solely or jointly with others, whether during regular business hours or otherwise; said Inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements shall become and remain the property of FRANKLIN whether or not patent applications or copyright, trademark, or maskwork registrations are filed thereon.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'EMPLOYEE' with 'WORKER' and changes the disclosure period from 'one year' to 'six months' after termination. The contradicted text uses the term 'EMPLOYEE' and states a disclosure period of 'one year'. This creates a direct contradiction because the disclosure periods are different (six months vs one year) and because if EMPLOYEE refers to WORKER, the Contradicted text then contradicts itself by indicating that the disclosure needs to occur after one year and after six months.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of FRANKLIN, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and any and all foreign countries, on said inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, without further consideration.
perturbation.changed_text: PERSONNEL shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of said PERSONNEL, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and any and all foreign countries, on said inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, without further consideration.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'EMPLOYEE' is replaced with 'PERSONNEL' and 'FRANKLIN' with 'PERSONNEL'. This creates a contradiction. The agreement states that FRANKLIN will bear the expense of patent applications, then changes it to 'Personnel' being responsible for their own expenses. This raises a dispute on who is responsible to pay the expenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of FRANKLIN, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and any and all foreign countries, on said inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, without further consideration.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'EMPLOYEE' with 'PERSONNEL' and 'FRANKLIN' with 'PERSONNEL'. This directly contradicts the original agreement, as it shifts the responsibility for patent application expenses from FRANKLIN to PERSONNEL. The contradicted text explicitly states that FRANKLIN will bear these expenses, while the changed text states PERSONNEL will bear the expenses. This constitutes a clear and direct financial inconsistency, aligning with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request of FRANKLIN execute all papers and do all other things that may be reasonably required in order to protect the rights of FRANKLIN, and to vest in FRANKLIN or its successors or assigns the entire rights, title and interest in and to any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, program improvements, and applications for letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations relating to anything pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN as provided above.
perturbation.changed_text: CONSULTANT shall from time to time, upon request of FRANKLIN execute all papers and do all other things that may be reasonably required in order to protect the rights of the CONSULTANT, and to vest in FRANKLIN or its successors or assigns the entire rights, title and interest in and to any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, program improvements, and applications for letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations relating to anything pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN as provided above.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'EMPLOYEE' is replaced with 'CONSULTANT' and the rights of FRANKLIN are switched to the rights of the CONSULTANT. This implies that the inventions and discoveries must protect the consultant rights, instead of franklin. This creates a contradiction on the main purpose of this contract. This raises a dispute on who is responsible to pay the expenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request of FRANKLIN execute all papers and do all other things that may be reasonably required in order to protect the rights of FRANKLIN, and to vest in FRANKLIN or its successors or assigns the entire rights, title and interest in and to any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, program improvements, and applications for letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations relating to anything pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN as provided above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'EMPLOYEE' with 'CONSULTANT' and shifts the protection of rights from 'FRANKLIN' to 'CONSULTANT'. The contradicted text refers to the original clause where the 'EMPLOYEE' protects the rights of 'FRANKLIN'. This creates a direct conflict because the altered text now mandates actions to protect the consultant's rights regarding inventions related to Franklin's business, contradicting the original intent of protecting Franklin's rights. The original and contradicted text are the same, emphasizing the introduced in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE further agrees not to divulge to any third party, either during his or her employment or thereafter, any confidential information conceived by him or her, disclosed by FRANKLIN, or obtained by him or her while in the employment of FRANKLIN relating in any way to any of FRANKLIN'S processes, businesses, customers, trade secrets, apparatus, products, software, packages, programs or trends in research, or to any of the inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements covered hereby, and agrees to maintain this information in confidence until such time that such information has become widely known to the public or described in an issued patent or in a printed publication of wide circulation. Upon termination of the employment, EMPLOYEE agrees to turn over to FRANKLIN all notes, memoranda, notebooks, drawings, records, customer lists, telephone files (including Rolodex and business card files) and correspondence in connection with anything done by him or her relating to his or her employment, for the reason that all confidential information contained therein is at all times the sole property of FRANKLIN. Following the termination of his or her employment with FRANKLIN, EMPLOYEE agrees not to solicit any employees or consultants of Franklin at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: CONTRACTOR further agrees not to divulge to any third party, either during his or her employment or thereafter, any confidential information conceived by him or her, disclosed by FRANKLIN, or obtained by him or her while in the employment of FRANKLIN relating in any way to any of FRANKLIN'S processes, businesses, customers, trade secrets, apparatus, products, software, packages, programs or trends in research, or to any of the inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements covered hereby, and agrees to maintain this information in confidence until such time that such information has become widely known to the public or described in an issued patent or in a printed publication of wide circulation. Upon termination of the employment, CONTRACTOR agrees to turn over to FRANKLIN all notes, memoranda, notebooks, drawings, records, customer lists, telephone files (including Rolodex and business card files) and correspondence in connection with anything done by him or her relating to his or her employment, for the reason that all confidential information contained therein is at all times the sole property of CONTRACTOR. Following the termination of his or her employment with FRANKLIN, CONTRACTOR agrees not to solicit any employees or consultants of Franklin at any time.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'EMPLOYEE' is replaced with 'CONTRACTOR' and the sole property of FRANKLIN is switched to sole property of the CONTRACTOR. This creates a contradiction since franklin is requiring the return of their confidential information, but agreeing that the contractor has the sole property of that information. There is a big disagreement on who is the sole owner of the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE further agrees not to divulge to any third party, either during his or her employment or thereafter, any confidential information conceived by him or her, disclosed by FRANKLIN, or obtained by him or her while in the employment of FRANKLIN relating in any way to any of FRANKLIN'S processes, businesses, customers, trade secrets, apparatus, products, software, packages, programs or trends in research, or to any of the inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements covered hereby, and agrees to maintain this information in confidence until such time that such information has become widely known to the public or described in an issued patent or in a printed publication of wide circulation. Upon termination of the employment, EMPLOYEE agrees to turn over to FRANKLIN all notes, memoranda, notebooks, drawings, records, customer lists, telephone files (including Rolodex and business card files) and correspondence in connection with anything done by him or her relating to his or her employment, for the reason that all confidential information contained therein is at all times the sole property of FRANKLIN. Following the termination of his or her employment with FRANKLIN, EMPLOYEE agrees not to solicit any employees or consultants of Franklin at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'EMPLOYEE' with 'CONTRACTOR' and states that confidential information is the sole property of the 'CONTRACTOR'. The contradicted text states the same confidential information is the sole property of 'FRANKLIN'. This creates a direct contradiction regarding ownership of confidential information, as both parties cannot simultaneously have sole ownership. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition as the changed text gives conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE understands and agrees that the continuance of EMPLOYEE in the service of FRANKLIN for a definite period is not made obligatory upon either party or a condition of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: TRAINEE understands and agrees that the continuance of EMPLOYEE in the service of FRANKLIN for a definite period is obligatory upon the party and a condition of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'EMPLOYEE' is replaced with 'TRAINEE' and 'not made obligatory' is replaced with 'obligatory'. The term employee is still being used and it is obligatory to continue this service. Since, both the trainee and employee are being referenced it's creating confusion on who is being held responsible for the obligations to continue this relationship with the company. This is an in-text contradiction and affects this section directly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE understands and agrees that the continuance of EMPLOYEE in the service of FRANKLIN for a definite period is not made obligatory upon either party or a condition of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the continuance of the EMPLOYEE (a term still used even though the text focuses on a TRAINEE) in the service of FRANKLIN is now obligatory, directly contradicting the contradicted text which explicitly states that it is 'not made obligatory'. This creates a direct and irreconcilable in-text contradiction regarding the obligation to continue service, leading to potential confusion and unenforceability. The definition of 'Misaligned Terminology' is being used, as both 'EMPLOYEE' and 'TRAINEE' are being referenced in the changed text, leading to conflicting obligations with the contradicted text.
